name: Herb-Packed Snapper
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a sharp knife, score the skin of the fillets several times. Spread the bean paste evenly over the skin. In a small bowl, combine the parsley and chives; sprinkle the herbs and flour evenly over the snapper. Place wax paper over the snapper and press down firmly. Set aside.
2. Cover the tomatoes with 1/2 cup boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain and reserve 1/4 cup liquid. Chop the tomatoes and set aside.
3. In a large nonstick skillet, heat 1 teaspoon of olive oil. Place the snapper in the skillet, skin-side down; cook for 4 minutes. Using a spatula, gently turn the fillets and cook for an additional 3 minutes, until cooked through. Remove the snapper to a plate and keep warm.
4. Stir the lemon juice and remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil into the skillet. Add the tomatoes and reserved liquid, pine nuts, and garlic; cook for 1 minute, until heated through. Spoon the sauce evenly over the snapper.
